name: leetcode-workflow
description: Claude Code marketplace for the leetcode-workflow plugin — systematic LeetCode practice tracking, all in git.

plugins.name: leetcode-workflow
plugins.description: Claude Code-native LeetCode practice tracker. Eight slash commands turn an empty git directory into a structured practice journal: scaffold problems from LeetCode URLs, time solves against per-difficulty thresholds, classify each solution against a closed pattern enum, and surface what to revisit (slow timing, brittle complexity, or stale past cooldown). Existing practice repos can be imported with one command. State lives in a SQLite database with five auto-generated Markdown views — progress, timings, retry queue, pattern coverage, history — all git-tracked and local-first. Mostly deterministic Python; Claude is invoked only where reasoning is genuinely needed (problem suggestion, pattern classification, body stripping for retry), keeping token cost low. Pedagogical contract enforced: Claude is a coach, not a solution generator — hints, complexity analysis, pattern names; never the answer.
